summary: Install-time credential and crypto-wallet exfiltration to hardcoded remote endpoint
details: The package ships postinstall.js wired as preinstall, postinstall, and main entry, so it executes automatically on `npm install` and on any `require` of the package. On execution it collects host identifiers (`os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo()`) and recursively walks the user's home directory (depth 3) looking for filenames matching a cryptocurrency wallet/seed keyword list (wallet.json, keystore.json, id.json, privatekey.json, seed.txt, mnemonic.txt, metamask.json, phantom.json, private.key, and similar) and.env /.env.local /.env.production files. It additionally reads ~/.ssh/id_* private keys, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.git-credentials, and ~/.npmrc (npm authToken), and POSTs the contents of each matched file, plus hostname and username, to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint http://107.161.90.180:7777 over plain HTTP. The package name `@wagni_bot/polygon-sdk` impersonates the Polygon blockchain SDK and ships an empty description with `main` pointing directly at the stealer, indicating the SDK framing is a lure to target blockchain developers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
